expectations.text: Recommends against directly injecting the public IFormSubmissionAppService across module boundaries
expectations.evidence: Response opens with 'Short answer: no, don't inject `IFormSubmissionAppService` into `ReportAppService`.' and dedicates the section 'Why not just inject `IFormSubmissionAppService`?' to explaining both failure modes.

expectations.text: Recommends ABP Integration Services as the right pattern, explicitly mentioning the [IntegrationService] attribute
expectations.evidence: Section 'The right pattern — Integration Services' shows the interface decorated with `[IntegrationService]` and the response notes 'The `[IntegrationService]` attribute is load-bearing.'

expectations.text: Defines the integration-service interface in the producing module's *.Application.Contracts (or *.Contracts) project
expectations.evidence: Code comment '// Acme.Forms.Application.Contracts/IFormSubmissionIntegrationService.cs' and prose 'Put this interface in `Acme.Forms.Application.Contracts`, alongside your normal `IFormSubmissionAppService`.'

expectations.text: Shows the implementation in the producing module's *.Application project (or equivalent), inheriting ApplicationService and implementing the [IntegrationService] interface
expectations.evidence: Code under 'Implement it in the producer's `*.Application` project' shows `public class FormSubmissionIntegrationService : ApplicationService, IFormSubmissionIntegrationService` in `Acme.Forms.Application/IntegrationServices/FormSubmissionIntegrationService.cs`.

expectations.text: Notes that integration services are hidden from the public API surface (and/or audit-off by default / not [Authorize] by default)
expectations.evidence: Bullets state: 'hidden from the public API surface (excluded from the regular `/api/...` Auto API Controllers and from the public Swagger doc)', 'Auditing is off by default', and '`[Authorize]` is not applied by default'.

expectations.text: Explains the in-process modular-monolith vs microservice transport difference (DI in-process now vs HTTP / generated proxy across services), framed as 'same contract, different transport'
expectations.evidence: Section 'In-process now vs cross-process later — same contract, different transport' contrasts 'Modular monolith' (DI resolves directly in-process) with 'Microservice deployment' (exposed over `/integration-api/...` with `abp generate-proxy`), explicitly noting 'The contract is the same; only the transport differs'.

expectations.text: Specifies that the consumer module's AbpModule depends only on the producer's *.Application.Contracts module (e.g. [DependsOn(typeof(AcmeFormsApplicationContractsModule))] on the Reports.Application module) — NOT on the producer's full *.Application module. This contracts-only dependency is the load-bearing detail that preserves the module boundary
expectations.evidence: Section 'The load-bearing piece — module dependency on Contracts only' shows `[DependsOn(typeof(AcmeFormsApplicationContractsModule), ... typeof(AcmeReportsDomainModule))]` on `AcmeReportsApplicationModule`, with explicit comments 'contracts only — RIGHT' and '// typeof(AcmeFormsApplicationModule), // full app module — WRONG', and prose 'The Reports application module `[DependsOn]`s **`AcmeFormsApplicationContractsModule` only** — never `AcmeFormsApplicationModule`.'
